reviews.text: We had delivery at the office and I got a chicken Caesar wrap. It's disgusting. The chicken is old and dry plus the greens are wilted to the point of almost being rotted. I ended up throwing out. Def not fresh food here
Cashier is not friendly. Staff spend more time talking to each other instead of paying attention to customers
Public bathroom is good and air conditioned
Great place to get a grilled cheese sandwich whilst waiting for the Staten Island Ferry to depart.

This use to be a garbage dump of a store and staff here were the rudest of the rude.  Prices are still too high, but this 5 stars is for the kind, friendly and professional service provided by Miss. Perla (sorry if misspelled) who provided a excellent service with a smile. Who ever the boss is, they are lucky to employ such a fantastic human being. Thanks Perla, your kind and professional service deserve more than 5 stars.

google_details.formatted_address: One Battery Park Plaza, Battery Pl, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Starbucks

reviews.text:
I waited over 10 minutes for the restroom, only to find there was no soap. When I told the cashier, she said it could be refilled in 5 minutes, but I’d need to wait in line again to use it.

I asked if I could use her hand sanitizer — she said they don’t have any in the kitchen. When I politely asked how staff clean their hands, she said “they use the restroom.”

Really, Starbucks? Shouldn’t your employees have access to hand sanitizer at all times — and shouldn’t your customers expect basic hygiene too?

Great place to find a bathroom and a place to charge my phone after getting off the Staten Island Ferry. Compared to the average Starbucks, the store seemed a little dirty with trash left on some tables or in a random corner during my visit. A decorative lamp had its lampshade askew. Staff seemed a little overwhelmed.

Perfect spot for after ferry ride to the Statue of Liberty in a very cold afternoon. It was quite busy and they had run out of everything we wanted to order so we ordered tea and 3 bagels. We got 2 teas and 3 cream cheeses with each bagel 😀. Luckily we got extras instead of missing. Another funny thing was we had given our name as “Aman”but they had noted it down as “Amam” and were calling us “Adam”…we had a fun break there. Most likely they are understaffed like most places so we had to clean our own table and make space to sit inside.

I ordered a Ribbon Crunch Frappuccino for the kids and a Caramel Frappuccino for me on a hot day with my family. Mine was not sweet at all and the taste was not what I expected. So I told the employee who made it that it was not sweet at all and handed it to her. She threw it into the trash can like a pitcher in front of me and sighed in displeasure. My son saw it too. I have never seen an employee like her at Starbucks. She was so rude and unfriendly.
Perfect sbux near battery park. Loved having a hot coffee with friends during the winter and couch on the backside is very cozy to take a break #beeyem 3* for worst restroom maintenance

google_details.name: Starbucks

reviews.text: It’s not my first time, I pay almost 6$ for Americano and little cream and there is literally no coffee in it.. it tastes like no Americano.. the quality here is so bad every time I buy here, the coffee is always too light .. it’s like they never put fresh beans in their expresso machines. So bad!!
Some of the staff members seem to be rude and not welcoming.
I love these guys, fast serving, smiling and kind. They made our breakfasts better. I would recommend a larger assortment of bakery for the breakfast.

Wish my experience was more pleasant with one of the staff members there. Unfortunately very rude customer service. She was very frustrated as I couldn’t hear her with the loud music and got my order with no name. Waiting for long minutes, she finally asked what I had ordered and told there was no more item. Unbelievable.

I was in today to make a purchase and because I had a $50 bill and my order was only $9.20 vs $10 they would not accept my cash.  I left without purchasing last time I checked all cash should be acceptable considering no signs were even posted.  Poor customer service seems to be there motto because they are Starbucks.  I am done with this franchise this city has many coffee shops with better coffee for reasonable prices.

google_details.formatted_address: PX2M+PR, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Pixie Scout Canteen

reviews.text: A nice cafe that is open only during the warm season. It is located inside the beautiful green Battery Park. We come here every summer to enjoy the view of the Statue of Liberty and sum up our personal year. I love this place.

My wife and I got drip coffee and a latte here, the staff were friendly and chatty, they were personable and still got us our drinks quickly. Can't comment on the food as we did not get any. The atmosphere of the area is beautiful and we'll located but is obviously seasonal and weather dependent.

Nice staff and decent iced coffee in a lovely spot. Unfortunately the only real outdoor food option at the Battery, made more unfortunate that the food was lackluster at best and spoiled at worst. Good for drinks in a pinch (though avoid the spritzer), but maybe step across the street for food.

We happened to be at the Battery and wanted coffee and snacks and decided to have bagels. It's certainly not cheap but it was convenient, and we resigned ourselves to paying a lot for only adequate refreshment. But we were actually really surprised by just how good both the coffee and bagels were! (And we've lived in NYC—we know that it's very possible to get very bad coffee and bagels there!) Service was about as good as it could be given that there was only one server on when we came, and she was dealing with several customers; she dealt with being a little pressured very well (and she was joined by someone else soon after). But it was the quality of the coffee and bagels that really bowled us over. Better than they had any business being from a random little tourist kiosk.
Perfect for a pit stop with the Supp team and intros to Pixie 🧡

reviews.text: Realizing that the cashier had messed up my order and it wasn't a small drink, it was a medium. He tells the guy who is making it to fix it so now I finish paying and now I'm watching the guy this man I literally. watched this man Watched him make it in a small cup and switch it to a medium cup and then add a whole bunch of more ice. He didn't realize I was standing behind him and then when I said something he's like no I'm gonna add more stuff. If we was going back-and-forth and then some other guy told him to throw it away, I'm not gonna do that. I said listen just forget it. He gives it to me. Still forgets that I have I'm supposed to get whipped cream on it and caramel Then he starts complaining the other guy about it because they both messed up and then of course, after all this, the cashier forgets my my donut I wouldn't recommend this place. The staff is very rude. I think I wrote a review before about one of the employees a lady being really rude and this is the man in the picture that try to just put it in another cup and was giving me attitude like I paid for this stuff. I don't understand why isn't an issue So no, I wouldn't go back and I go to Dunkin' Donuts like almost every day

I used the Dunkin’ Donuts app to order my food while I was on the ferry. I get there thinking my food would be available for me to snatch and go because I was trying not to be late for work. There was a bag on the counter unlabeled, no attached receipt with order number NOTHING. I asked one of the girls making food about my order because I noticed she’s calling numbers after mine. She nodded her head and said she’s making it. Comes back to call another number not mine. I go to the register and ask for a manager because at this point waiting 15 minutes was ridiculous (I wanted a refund). The guy walks from the register and said oh this bag that been sitting here is yours . I said how was I suppose to know if it’s not labeled. He then passed it to me . I said I don’t want that it been sitting there and multiple ppl picked it up looking inside to see if it was theirs. I requested a new sandwich…they act as if they was frustrated for me wanting a fresh hot sandwich which I paid for. Long story short I was late and recommend you not to use the app for this location because they don’t label the food like they’re suppose too and there is NEVER a manager to deal with bad service.

We were lucky to come across Dunkin Donuts at the Staten Island’s Ferry Terminal while waiting for the ferry, as we were feeling a bit hungry. We bought six donuts and they tasted fresh and delicious. We got four with rainbow sprinkles and two plain glazed ones. Yummy!

I urgently needed to charge my phone and a guy named Ashraf helped me out. During the waiting time, he surprised me pleasantly with the friendly atmosphere that he and his colleagues created. Really appreciated, and a recommended go-to place!

I ordered a coffee and had a very bad experience. The staff started arguing with some random person instead of doing their job. I waited 10–15 minutes, and ended up late for work. Very unprofessional team and no respect for customers.

Perfekt für einen Snack auf die Hand, wenn man unterwegs

Personne très aimable

reviews.text: No price tags on anything so it feels like he just makes up a price and the guy was so rude, he didn't say one word to me, but rather when he wanted me to TAP my card started pounding on the card reader and expected me to know what he meant. It felt really creepy.

It costs too much, but I guess that's expected in a ferry terminal. They recently shrank the size of drinks that comes with the special; and even when you pay for a large- it ain't large. But gosh darn it, as a NYer, sometimes a crisp hot dog and a papaya drink is what you need. I keep going back, so I can't ding it too much.
Probably the greatest meal I’ve ever had. I proposed to my wife here. We didn’t stay together but the food was EPIC!!!

i’ve always loved this place but… over $7 for only four chicken strips??? bffr. i used to go here for the homerun special (two hotdogs and a small papaya drink for $5) but with these new prices since covid i’d much rather go to literally any of the other eateries at the ferry terminal. RIP len’s papaya 😞🙏🏽
Do Not recommend buying a lot of Pizza at same time they always charge you more... Even explain to them they always denied it, but the Pizza is very good...

reviews.text: The Dave's single was very good  and fresh. Customer service was also good. There are no seats but you could probably sit in the departure and arrivals seats of the Staten Island ferry located right next to the Wendy's.

This Wendy’s has significantly declined. Service is incredibly slow, and the food quality has deteriorated considerably. To add insult to injury, they recently implemented a substantial price increase. For instance, their large seasoned potato wedges, a staple for my morning routine, have jumped from $2.60 to a shocking $4.35. Consequently, I no longer consider this my go-to breakfast spot.

Always get something to eat for me and my kid and sadly this place is what he wants. The food never hot or fresh and the amount of food that's inside the carton is horribly low. I dont understand how the small portion of food cost so much. They getting everyone that's getting on the boat, run tourist. Price and food amount does not match.
Really long hair. One of the workers was just playing with some of the sandwiches.
Great service, very kind employees

google_details.name: NAYA

reviews.text: I dined here a few days ago and tried their falafel bowl with rice and I really loved it! The falafel was very delicious, even though it wasn’t freshly made or warm. The bowl also came with a small pita bread on the side, which was a nice touch. The portion size was generous and filling. Overall, a great value and tasty meal. Highly recommend!

This is my second time experienced buying my bowl without the pita bread being available. I specifically asked face to face prior to placing my order if they still have the bread I was told they do. After I paid my order and then told there’s no more left.  Very upset !!!

It’s really not worth $14. The store manager was nice enough to add some rice but this is outrageous to have half a box filled up. When I asked them the first time, they confidently said that’s enough of chicken shawarma. Really disappointed as this used to be my go to place for lunch. Sadly won’t be returning here.

I am writing about the Naya in financial district near battery park. Food is fresh and good. You can get with meat or without meat and they have cauliflower and falafel for non-meat proteins. You can have a base of salads or grains or bread. You then pick your protein and you can get all the vegetarian add-ons that you want. You can customize it your way and have all possible combination you want. We tried their falafel salad and Chicken swharma  wrap with the jalapeño sauce and was amazing. If you are in New York must try there wraps

Great food. Can't go wrong with anything here and is a nice mix up from the usual bowl places like chipotle.

One thing you can't skip here is the green jalapeno sauce. Perfect sauce for the pita on the side.

Only downside of this place is because it's so popular the line can get kind of long during lunch. Other than that no complaints whatsoever.

reviews.text: They make salads and wraps. I had the southwestern steak wrap. The wrap itself was packed, they did not skimp out on the ingredients. It was tasty with a a sweet spicy sauce that added flavor. Customer service was good  and the place itself was very clean.

I found a ladybug in my warm bowl, which was incredibly disappointing. I'd already eaten two bites, and the thought of it ruined my entire lunch experience. It's made me wonder what else I might have unknowingly consumed in the past, and I felt weird the whole day.

Finding this spot makes me want to try more unknown places. Their cooking produces great flavors, relies on freshness, and creates beautiful plates. Excellent staff attention with delicious cuisine at moderate costs. The workers recognize me immediately when I return. This place creates lasting food memories.

Yelp users steered me toward one of my best meals this month. Their workers were both knowledgeable and personable. Quality ingredients don't lead to high prices. Each dish represents quality ingredients. This restaurant has my heart and will capture yours too.
Ash-Marie is the best Chopt worker I've ever seen. She's really helpful and has a great personality. Always greeting with a smile, really like the vibe of the store

reviews.text: Great dog friendly patio bar in Liberty Park. $5 hot dogs $8 burgers and beers.

Appears to be permanently closed.
I’ve come here three times in the last month during hours when they claim to be open and they’re closed each time. 😕

This place has the worst hours.
It’s a fall afternoon. The park is packed. The weather is beautiful and it’s closed.
There has never been a time when I wanted to go here and it was open. NEVER
It's a great place to have a quick drink or bite. The hotdogs and burgers are ok. Do not expect to have a view of the rest of the park

Worst place ever! Rude, unfriendly stuff. A burger, smaller than a Cheeseburger at McDonalds costs 9$. It‘s dry and not good. The caesars salad with grilled chicken is ice cold and the salad is not fresh for 8$...
You better go to Wendy‘s at the Ferry station. I would give 0 Stars if it would be able! Don‘t go here for anything else then drinking a beer.. and also for that, there are better locations...

google_details.name: Perros y Vainas - The Battery

reviews.text: First time trying a Venezuelan hot dog, and it was delicious! I had The Big Mess—totally worth it. It’s a convenient spot to grab a bite, especially after getting back from the Liberty Island excursion. Great location and tasty food!

Great location just off the SI Ferry terminal. Price wise very friendly for the location. Nice outdoor seating areas with tables, chairs and sofas.
Food has flavors and kids loved them.

Amazing food and service from Anayancy! We had the pleasure of meeting the owner too, she made sure everyone was having a great experience! We had the cheese empanadas, tequeños (cheese sticks) and the pupusa peluda (braised beef with yellow cheese). Wow amazing! We also had live music and there is an exhibition too in virtual reality about the Venezuelan sightseeing wonders. Highly recommend the visit!

Absolutely loved Perros y Vainas in New York!
I stopped by and tried the tequeños — and wow, they were amazing! Perfectly crispy on the outside and deliciously cheesy inside. Honestly, everything on the menu looked great, and the food I tried was full of flavor and clearly made with care. The vibe is fun, the service is friendly, and it’s the kind of place you’ll want to come back to. Highly recommend if you’re craving something tasty and different!

The first time that I came they made me wait like 30-35 min for 3 empanadas, and today I placed an order for pickup from Uber eats and by 5:20 when I got there, they was closed already. And I had to deal with the customer service to ask them to cancel the order. And I left the restaurant already and they made me come back to take a clear picture to prove that the restaurant was closed. Please FIX your schedule. And I’m not going back to this restaurant because what happened to me today .

AMAZING experience, my burrrito came out so good with Evelyn! Thnkuuu for the delicious food!

We made a catering order from this location. I’m truly grateful for the excellent customer service received. I ordered online and their website is user-friendly. The food is amazingly plentiful and delicious. The order arrived just in time for the all attorney meeting. The feedback received was outstanding! This is a restaurant that’ll make you look good when catering! Excellence at its best! I’m thinking burritos for next time 😋

We’re so grateful to Aaliyah, Roberta, and Evelyn for helping us cater an amazing meal for our film crew after a long day of shooting! They went above and beyond to make sure everything was fresh, organized, and beautifully presented. The whole team appreciated the care and kindness!!
Great Mexican food with amazing views of the Manhattan downtown. The sitting area was good and comfortable. Their hot sauce was really spicy.

reviews.text: Not recommended - the ice and custard was not tasting good.
Very good ice cream! I love it ♥️
I love the frozen custard in summertime especially because it is buy 1 get 1 free on Wednesdays

It’s always difficult to find an employee- and they never seem to know the recipes for anything on the menu. Today, when I walked up, there was a long line of people who were asking where the employees were. They walked out and disappeared into the back again. One of them returned, and was very slow to service customers. I ordered the strawberry shortcake concrete- which apparently is a mystery on what goes in it. She asked her colleague- who had to tell her where to find the ingredients. I told her that since I was trying to catch the ferry, that she didn’t have to blend it, since I was worried it would take even longer to figure out. I expected to receive strawberry flavor between 2 layers of custard with some type of graham cracker or crunchy aspect on top (it was called strawberry shortcake.) when she was done- it was basically a half done sundae- not worth the almost $8. I looked up an example of what a concrete should look like… night and day. I definitely overpaid. Next time I treat myself, I’ll go elsewhere. Train the staff and post the recipes.
Awesome place, friendly staff and super tasty !! Highly recommend after the beach.

reviews.text: Right outside the Staten Island Ferry convenient to Wall Street means that this is halal meant for locals not tourists.  My lamb falafel and chicken hit the spot after cycling all day!
It was amazing! I loved the food and the guy was very chill and friendly
Good food. It's relatively cheaper compared to other halal foods around the area, and it really fills your stomach.
Amazing. Don't stop at other "halal" carts that sell hot dogs, go to the real deal and get some amazing authentic food!
Good food but very small portion size.

google_details.formatted_address: 4 South St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Pizza Plus

reviews.text: This is a perfect spot for a refreshing treat! Their Italian ice is always smooth, flavorful, and packed with real fruit taste, while the frozen custard is rich and creamy. The staff is friendly and efficient, ensuring quick service even on busy days. Whether you're craving a classic gelati or trying a new flavor, Rita’s never disappoints. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a sweet and satisfying dessert.

Jashim was amazing. Great customer service. The pizza tasty and the ice cream was the perfect treat after leaving Battery Park / Statute of Liberty cruise! Now to the subway and heading to Time Square!
Jashim has great customer service. He smiled and made sure we was happy with our order. My pizza was fresh and full of flavor. I’ll definitely visit again

I got served by Jeshim and I must say, as a Belgian tourist, I was blown away that pizza from a transit station cornershop would taste this good, like, I did NOT see that coming. Would've liked a bit more sitting space, but the pizza was GREAT. New York City truly does not lack when it comes to pizza!

We tried a slice at 4 different pizza places in NY (Manhattan). This was by far the best tasting pizza we had.  If it's location wasn't so far from everything else we were doing I definitely would've gone back.  I got a meat lovers slice. It should've been called bacon lovers cause there was so much hubby got a Buffalo chicken that was also done perfectly.  Only downfall of NYC pizza is they haven't seemed to have heard of dill pickle pizza.  Hopefully the owner will see this and give it a shot cause if any of the pizza places can do it right it would be this one.

Dominic ran The truck for 30 years and now  John is running it for at least 25. It’s always at the same spot just takes cash and is the best sausage and peppers and onions you will ever have

I had a great experience. The staff was friendly, and the service was quick. I ordered the Marketplace Vegetables Bowl, it was fresh, flavorful, and satisfying. I highly recommend stopping by if you’re in downtown Manhattan!
Room service done right!!! Finally a hotel that has something for everyone in my family! Delicious food and great customer service!
So good

google_details.name: Wafels & Dinges

reviews.text:
This is located right next to the subway exit/entrance and we were just hungry so we decided to try here. It’s a cute little place but it was okay overall.
My waffle was $12 I think and it was so small….We used the kiosk to put order in and My bf wanted to order mimosas but it wasn’t on the kiosk machine menu….later this lady also wanted to order mimosa and she asked the staff but she was told to use the kiosk to order and of course Mimosa is not on the kiosk menu even though they advertised to have mimosa…..
Lemonade was good but $12 for the small waffle is overpriced…I know it’s new work but probably I wouldn’t come back for waffles.

What a gem! Perfect respite for a hot August day. Tahitian vanilla with a dash of cinnamon, yummy. Cookies and cream made with gingerbread Cookies, sublime!
Chatted with the very friendly owner, great fellow. And the counter staff where so accommodating.

The owner was nice enough to come and say hi to us while we were eating to ask how everything was. They got one item wrong and they made sure that they rotted it before we left. Definitely worth checking out when you come here. I had the strawberry shortcake plus four more for  my family.

Had to wait 10mims for 4x ladies to decide what they want to order from the single automated terminal. I ask if I could order directly at the counter, which I was told no. Then another lady ordered a drink at the counter. Eventually ordered at the counter as the automated terminal failed. They served cookies and cream when I asked for salted caramel. Not a good experience.
Pretty cafe in yellow! Took a stop here to try some waffles before heading out to the statue.

google_details.formatted_address: 4 South St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Auntie Anne's

reviews.text: Be careful of this place. While paying for the food, after I used my card the tip question popped up and cashier just turned screen towards her and picked 10% tip for me. Also she was trying to be slick and when she was telling me the price for the food, she stated $…. with taxes. It hit me later that she already gave me the price with 10% tip added.

Stop by last night for a to go pick up, got 2 cups one with pretzel dogs and one with the classic salted nuggets. Both were delicious and fresh. The employee who helped me was very nice. He suggested the flavors since I was indecisive. I had a great experience and would definitely go back. The nuggets were worth the trip.

My wife used to live in Chicago and when in Chicago she used to eat Auntie Ann's nuggets pretty often. She then made me discover them and we both were quite fond of them. I say "were" because, the other day, waiting to board the shuttle to Staten Island, we bought a bucket but didn't get what we expected at all. Some of the nuggets were chewy, others were completely dry. The taste was still the same but eating them like that was not pleasant at all.
The shop was just opening so maybe they served the ones from the day before, which would be unacceptable... or maybe they're just bad now... I don't know which option would be the worst.
What I know is that I'm not sure I want to give them another try... 😥
One thing was good though: the whole bucket was something like $6 and it was enough for the 3 of us... But it does not matter that it's cheap if the overall experience is bad.

Quality & taste: 3/10
Quantities: 9/10
Atmosphere: n/a
Originality: 7/10
Ratio price / quality: 4/10
Service: 3/10
How I felt afterwards: 3/10

More on IG @runnerandfoodie

The employee serving me was rude, shaking her head and mumbling because I was moving to slow with the machine and pressing the botton for that tip. Date  Sat 28th June at 11:58pm. A Hispanic young lady.  There was no one else in the line behind me. I told her no need to be so rude. And she responded she didn't care. I am never buying there again and would tell my friends and family to also not buy, because I take the ferry everyday. Maybe only the tourist gets respect, a smile and a thank you. Remember the locals held you down and supported that business during COVID.

my girlfriend dropped her fork twice on the floor, she asked one of the workers for another fork and he looked at her and walked away….very rude and sick….will never go there again…… really weird….. CANT recommend!!!!!!!!

google_details.formatted_address: 32 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Hampton Inn Manhattan/Downtown-Financial District

reviews.text:
This hotel was our "home base" during our recent vacation to Southern Manhattan and provided us with everything we needed for our stay.
I selected the Hampton Inn due to its location for easy access to the ferries for Governors Island, restaurants, transportation, stores, and many landmarks. Since I have to travel with medication that requires refrigeration, I called in advance and spoke with the front desk agent, Q, who informed me that refrigerators are not standard in the rooms but can be requested. She graciously placed a notion on our reservation and I was already pleased with my first interaction. On the day of our arrival, I called again to confirm a mini fridge was placed in the room and had the pleasure of speaking with Jonathan who assured me one should be there, and if not, to call down for one to be brought up.
Upon arrival, we were welcomed by Jonathan who was friendly, efficient, thorough during check in allowing us some must needed rest after traveling all morning. Although there isn't a water fountain in the lobby, the juice machine in the breakfast area offers water. In the concessions area, the usual selection of snacks and drinks are available for purchase, and there is complimentary coffee, tea, and hot chocolate available 24/7. The gym is located in the basement but I didn't venture down there having gotten all my exercise from exploring the Big Apple. The breakfast area provides free hot breakfast for all guests with a selection of breads, muffins, some style of egg, protein, and a side, along with their waffle station and "oatmeal" which was in fact cream of wheat. I was disappointed by the extremely limited selection of gluten-free items and the quality of what was provided was mediocre.
The room was comfortable and the air conditioning was powerful, welcomed relief from the late September heat. The mini fridge was in the room when we arrived and I appreciated the staff's cooperation to have that request fulfilled. The bathroom had a sliding barn door, while en vogue, are more of a nuisance to open and close but the real disappointment was the one hook on the wall. This was inadequate for two damp towels to be hung and one kept ending up on the floor since the only option was to sling it across the small shelf since there were no handles on the shower door or any other option.
Overall, we enjoyed our visit and would stay here again when concentrating our vacation in this neighborhood of Manhattan. I recommend it for business travelers due to its proximity to the Financial District and to tourists looking to take advantage of the numerous ferries and subway stations. A huge thank you to all the wonderfully helpful staff: Q, Jonathan, and another gentleman who worked at night and always greeted us with a smile. We look forward to coming back.

Front Desk assign us a room at the lower floor just facing the street, despite us requesting for a higher floor. Hot shower was not hot and the room feels like i travelled back to the 70s with antique furniture. Value for money 0. the only saving grace was that it was near the statue of liberty and wall street.

**This is solely my opinion from my personal experience**. My wife and I had a very unsatisfactory stay at this hotel during my birthday weekend. We found the building infested with roaches and some kind of rat, which was disturbing. My vehicle was also damaged at the Hilton-recommended valet parking garage, incurring a significant repair cost, despite us having paid the parking fees to Hilton. I won't get into the details. The hotel management did not assist, and we never received an apology for the experience. I cannot recommend this particular Hilton-branded hotel. If you're looking for a safe place to stay when you visit NYC, I would not recommend this hotel. Unfortunately, the hotel management did not show accountability or take care of their customers (which is not typical of a Hilton).

Stayed here for 1 night while in New York for business.  Centrally located in the Financial District, just a few blocks from the Charging Bull and the New York Stock Exchange.  It's a small hotel that's basically built vertically, but has all the perks and amenities of other Hampton Inn's.

Front Desk staff were all very friendly during check-in, throughout the stay, and at checkout.  Room was clean and had everything I needed for a business stay.  They have a free continental breakfast every morning, but there's plenty of places for food and drinks within a walking distance.  I'll be staying here again on my next upcoming trips.

This hotel is in a perfect location for exploring lower Manhattan, Governor's Island and for catching ferries. Shout out to Jonathan for taking care of the return of some clothing items we left in our room. After trying to arrange this myself, he made it happen, and at the hotel's expense. This is above and beyond. Everyone we interacted with was friendly and helpful.  Our room was comfortable, accommodated 3 without issue, and elevators worked well (something rare in these older buildings). Like any stay, some things could be improved, but looking back, our trip was a blast and the hotel and staff helped make our trip a real win.  The morning breakfast was good, with a decent selection and I didn't see anything run our during our stay. Great job! I would return here again for a future stay.

google_details.formatted_address: 6 Water St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Hilton Garden Inn NYC Financial Center/Manhattan Downtown

reviews.text: The front reception desk attendant was friendly. The overall entry and lobby areas were nice. Once I got to my room (# 603) it was overall good and the bed, floors, etc. were clean. However, the bathroom was not acceptable. First this room was a handicap room and I don’t need that. I do not have a physical disability. This room should have been saved for someone else who needs it. Honestly though no one should be using it until things are better and fixed. The shower had black mold in the corner caulking. The shower head, even with adjusting, was subpar. Hot water was scolding hot or barely tolerable or cold. I was curious to why there was a stack of 3 extra bath towels on the bed when I got to the room. Now I know why. The shower was a transition-less, flat entry for ADA reasons and it’s understandable given that’s the purpose of the room. However water did not slope and drain to stay in the slippery shower floor (not ADA compliant). No, the water slipped out and into the overall bathroom floor. Sure there was an extra drain hole in the bathroom floor near the shower entry (so 2 drain holes), but it didn’t actually drain. The slope is not acceptable for either drain. I had to use all 3 of those extra towels to mop up the excess bathroom floor water. Seriously what in the world — yeah, not ADA compliant. I’m blessed to not have a physical disability but if I did and traveling alone there would be seriously dangerous possibilities using this bathroom. I will never stay at this hotel again for this reason.

What a perfect place to stay in Manhattan. I thoroughly enjoyed this hotel.

Easy access to cabs and Uber.

Easy access to food, walking distance to simply delicious options.

Beautiful water views.

Walking distance of Ferry.

Great service.

Easy access to Wall Street.

Restaurant in building on 2nd Floor.

Highly recommend ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Quiet, quaint, convenient.

Had such an amazing time here. It's so close to battery park and to subways as well, which makes it really convenient to travel NYC. We were even able to see the 4th of July fireworks from our window (albeit obstructed by a building), but watching the fireworks from the comfort of our room, without dealing with the crowds was such a dream. The staff at the reception were really nice and friendly too. Thanks for the awesome service!

Quieter location in FiDi. About 30 min walk or train ride to LES or midtown.

Hotel is just average for a Hilton.  Rooms are small and tight. The phone didn’t work in the room and the shower had lousy pressure and leaked outside the shower itself.  The lighting is so dim in the room and it’s very dark in the side we were on.  Asked for another room but they did not accommodate.
They say happy hour 4-7  but really there’s no happy hour- just means bar in the 2nd floor is open and you pay for what you want.  Staff is nice generally.  I would not stay here if you plan to spend any time in your room. It’s only for sleeping and taking a showet.

The hotel stay was okay but nothing particularly special. The room was clean but the breakfast was very disappointing — long waits (30-40mins), always run out of coffee, clearly understaffed and tables often not cleaned. On the positive side, the location is very convenient, with plenty of good restaurants and tourist attractions nearby. It’s a decent place to stay if you value location.

google_details.name: Michelangelo hotel

google_details.formatted_address: 90 Broad St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Dunkin'

reviews.text: I ordered an iced tea.Thw tea came out rancid as if the machine hadn't been cleaned in a while. I asked for a refund but they offered me another tea instead. I asked them to taste their own tea ( from their own cup) which they _refused_. When I insisted they either taste it or give me a refund they gave me my money back. They need to clean their machines. I won't be going back.

This place is TERRIBLE!!!

The few times I've gone have been me HOPING service and the food would be better that day, but it never is!

The last two times I've visited,  I ordered loaded hash browns. Both times,  I received a few dry, burnt, reheated, hash browns in a coffee cup,  with a drop of sauce, and half of a bacon that wasn't chopped up. There was NOTHING loaded about it!!! Both times I've trashed it!

I will NEVER waste my money here again. Buyer BEWARE!!!!

They have pumpkin donuts!!! I am so 🔥 because yo boy is a pumpkin fan.  I love their pumpkin (where is the pumpkin emoji) drink as well, Nazmin was my server, she was great, visited this location today (Sept 6th) at about 750am.

It was one of the most pleasant experiences of visiting the place with donuts. I ordered coffee, my friends, ordered donuts and we had a great time in the park not far from this place. Cashier improved my mood. Thanks

Absolutely horrendous service. Only drinks are made screw the rest of your order. Was given a bacon egg and cheese with no bacon. Didn’t realize until I got to my office. They always have issues and will get upset with you if you tell them that you’re missing an item. People were waiting for over 10 mins for their order while other people were getting their things before them. I waited about 20 mins for a mobile order that I put in 10 mins before even getting to this store. I understand the other Dunkin is closed so there are a lot more people at this location but they’ve had issues even before that happened!!!!!!

google_details.formatted_address: 85 Broad St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Le Pain Quotidien

reviews.text: It is a nice breakfast place with fresh bread and pastries. It’s a bit pricier than other spots around here, but the vibe is definitely upscale. I went for the avocado toast with smoked salmon added on, and it was super tasty! It came with some veggies and a zesty lemon dressing that went really well with it. Plus, the coffee was delicious too!

Tried the French Cream Doughnut because it looked beautiful and was highly recommended online. Unfortunately, it was a huge letdown. The cream lacked flavor and the bread was dense and unremarkable. At over $7, it felt overpriced for something I don’t even want to finish. Nice for photos, but not for eating. Staff also just mind their own business.

The first time we went here I would have given this a 5/5. Service was amazing! Our waiter was fantastic and our food was great. So good we decided to come back.

Second time our waiter was disgruntled and came by only when flagged down. Most of the time we couldn’t find him and had to flag down servers from behind the bar to get more water. I never got a refill of my coffee. All our food and coffee came out cold. We ordered a large mocha and got a small. My wife ordered a pastry that never came… It was a true 180 from the first experience.

1/1 can’t recommend you go here based off of the second experience. Really disappointed with the experience, service, and food the second go around. Everything about the second experience was painful.

Very tasty food!
And at the same time the prices are huge! 16$ for bread with avocado without salmon without eggs is too much! Even with that exterior and atmosphere.
Common guys. Make prices more reasonable.
And they put little strawberry sauce in my matcha. If you make quality - make quality.
And the even don’t allow to sit in the dining zone! I payed 17$ for small corner. Nice.

Server forgot one of our coffees ( we were a table of 2). And the place wasn’t very crowded.
Gingerbread pecan crookie was good, a little dry: 5/10
Apple crumble cheesecake was good. 7/10

I expected more for the price

google_details.formatted_address: 20 Stone St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Cafe Grumpy - Financial District

reviews.text: The espresso was decent and the space here was nice inside. The staff were helpful and friendly. The pastry was stale and hard, I didn’t even finish it (and I was pretty hungry). The coffee here is okay but I can’t really recommend the pastries here.
It really worth to stop here and grab your coffee, like the quality, conversation with the team, they even know what type of beans they brew 👌🏻

Great stop for a coffee. Enjoyed the cortado and they don’t charge extra for milk alternatives (such as oat, almond, soy) which is amazing! Indoor tables available to sit in. The service was very fast even with a line. Some pastries available - the almond croissant and scone looked good!

this place has some of the strongest coffee in the city. my dad got an americano, and he had low expectations given he drinks insanely strong coffee... but he was pleasantly surprised! the coffee was smooth and strong, not bitter at all. i got their honey latte, and it was great! im not a coffee expert, so i can't personally say much, but it's very solid. i definitely recommend a visit here!

Snug coffee shop in Financial District. On weekends, this place is spacious with a cozy seating area. (I haven’t been there during the week, so maybe it’s a different situation then?) Service is top quality - the lates here are delightful!

I don’t recall there being a bathroom at this location.

google_details.formatted_address: 10 South St Slip, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Joe Coffee Company: Battery Maritime

reviews.text: We were really regretful having not had her morning coffee before a trip to Governors Island in Manhattan. But luckily, Joe’s coffee saved the day and we were able to get our fix. This is a small serving counter that is just what you need before heading to the ferry.

Joe Coffee is wonderful and I’m a fan in general, but the quality control at this outpost is slipshod. Our lattes tasted almost burnt and were close to undrinkable. I would dearly love the Joe leadership to do a quality audit here, as I worry this location could damage the brand. I will continue to visit other Joe locations gladly - but not this one.
Good coffee. For the love of god, please CLEAN payment screen. It’s filthy.

Ordered an iced latte with four shots but he poured the espresso out of a large jug from the fridge instead of the espresso machine. It tasted nothing like a latte — pretty sure he just gave me black coffee with milk.
The sweetest baristas work here! I’m always surprised how delicious their cold brew is and it helps that the people making the drink are kind and patient. Loyal customer!!

evidence_reviews.text: The sweetest baristas work here! I’m always surprised how delicious their cold brew is and it helps that the people making the drink are kind and patient. Loyal customer!!

google_details.formatted_address: 66 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: 787 Coffee

reviews.text: What an amazing Cafe this is in the lower downtown Manhattan. Coffee, Food, Ambiance, and above all the customer service all is super amazing. Javier has to be the coolest and most helpful barista who not only guided on selecting the perfect coffee but got us to modify the sweetness level as well. The place is done so well and has all types of seatings available. They also have electric charging slots as well as USB and C type outlets. They have a nice washroom as well. This place is quite popular for people to work on their laptops. They also have in-house printing, and copying facility with a printer. This is a really nice spot on your way to and back from the Staten Island ferry as well. A great coffee spot and almost part of the New York Coffee experience.

Love love love. Directly supports Puerto Rican farmers and artists. Delicious coffee and empanadas. Horchata and Dulce de Leche lattes were superb. I miss this place already and will be back. The art and stickers are all really cool too. Bought a shirt for my artist son to take home to Cali.

Out-of-this-world coffee.  Friendly, helpful and informative service.   Wonderful coffee/café vibe, with a very welcoming inclusive mood.  Hilarious commentary throughout the shop.

Now the coffee - mind blowing good!  I had the whiskey ..."bathed in oak barrels, allowing them to absorb the velvety goodness of whiskey, creating a unique coffee experience that is both fragrant and truly special".  Direct quote because I cannot improve this description.  As an avid scotch drinker, this was a marriage of whiskey and coffee that I will remember.

This is a must visit and experience.

This place is AMAZING!!! The service, the coffee and food are 10000000/10. The staff is so kind and knowledgeable. Since it was my first time they took the time to walk me through their different Puerto Rican coffee beans and I ended up getting the Coquito latte. The coffee was PHENOMENAL. I also tried their signature empanada and it did not disappoint. So delicious, savory, and light! The perfect snack to compliment my coffee! The atmosphere was so cozy and inviting! I ended up staying a while and grabbed another empanada! The stickers and merch are worth checking out as well! I ended up getting the sticker pictured. Couldn’t say enough good things about this place! Every time I’m in the city I will be making a point to stop here!

Just visited 787 Coffee on Pearl St in NYC - two days in a row because I loved it so much! From the Puerto Rican flag 🇵🇷 flying out front to the chill vibe and super friendly staff, everything about this place is special. The Coquito and Horchata lattes were amazing, the chicken empanadas delicious, and I even took home a bag of NuevaYol Coffee. From coffee farmers in Puerto Rico to roasting and serving their own single-origin coffee with love ❤️ — please bring a 787 to Miami! ☕️

google_details.formatted_address: 81 Pearl Street, 48 Stone St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Beckett's Sports Bar

reviews.text:
If you’re in the Wall Street area and looking for a good bite, this spot’s a solid choice! It’s got a ton of space with two separate bars spread over two floors. We popped in during October and it was super chill—definitely not crowded at all.

We went for the bangers and mash and the Beckett smash burger, and let me tell you, that burger was bomb! I asked for pepper jack cheese instead of American, but I think the kitchen totally forgot because I ended up with American. No biggie, though! Our waiter was really cool about it and even hooked me up with some jalapeño slices as an apology. Now that’s what I call great customer service.

I almost forgot, the fries were super delicious!

No reservations were necessary; good for a big company as well as a for a couple to step by. Pros were big portions can fill you up, a short wait time, friendly waitress.
Cons were: close seats to others, no smokers  were  separated from non-smokers.
Good for a lunch and/ or dinner when you are strolling in a downtown. Salmon was fine and my husband’s steak was well done and delicious. Cesar salad was lacking croutons and a fries from sweet potato were a hit.
Great bar, good food and service. Relatively quiet compared to other places in New York, especially for the football (soccer) game. Recommended.

I’m not a bar guy And I only go here because sometimes I go to a men’s gathering led by my church. I just wanted to say that the service was great. I felt very welcomed and cared for by Siobhan, she is really awesome. I look forward to coming back and hopefully seeing Siobhan!

Laid back, all the football games on with red zone & sound (hard to find anywhere these days so we definitely appreciated the sports bar atmosphere)! Friendly cool bar staff. I really liked this spot for watching football on a Sunday afternoon. They have some game day bar deals. Average food and value, but we had a fun time watching games here. Thanks to the staff. Would recommend.

google_details.formatted_address: 52 Stone St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Stone Street Tavern

reviews.text:
Stone Street is one of those hidden gems in New York City that feels like you’ve stepped into another world. Tucked away in the Financial District, this historic cobblestone street is lined with bars, pubs, and restaurants, all with tables spilling out into the street. We stopped by for lunch and enjoyed two beers in the sun, and it was the perfect break from the hustle and bustle of Manhattan.

The atmosphere is unique – it almost feels European, with its narrow street, outdoor seating, and lively mix of locals, office workers, and tourists. Even at midday, the energy was great without being overwhelming. The service was friendly, the beers were cold, and it’s just a fantastic place to sit back, relax, and soak in the vibe of lower Manhattan.

Stone Street is especially worth visiting if you’re already in the area, for example exploring Battery Park, Wall Street, or the 9/11 Memorial. It’s not just about one bar – it’s the whole setting that makes it so special. Whether you’re looking for a casual drink, a hearty lunch, or just a place to people-watch, Stone Street delivers. A true New York experience that feels both historic and modern at the same time.

Nice spot in a historic part of the city. Stopped by for a late lunch and was pleasantly surprised. The Hangover Burger, topped with bacon, cheddar, and a fried egg, was delicious. They offer a good variety of craft beers, and the mac & cheese was rich and satisfying. Service was prompt and friendly. A great place to relax and enjoy hearty comfort food in the Financial District.

Service was great, all the servers were friendly and attentive.
Food was amazing; especially the tuna tartare, all the ingredients were fresh and the tuna was juicy.
I went for drinks and apps and it was standard Tavern food.  I loved the giant pretzel.  Drinks were also good

The waitress was friendly and knowledgeable of the beer selection.  I had the Weihenstephaner and it was delicious! German beer always do it right.
Cheers 🍻

evidence_reviews.text:
Stone Street is one of those hidden gems in New York City that feels like you’ve stepped into another world. Tucked away in the Financial District, this historic cobblestone street is lined with bars, pubs, and restaurants, all with tables spilling out into the street. We stopped by for lunch and enjoyed two beers in the sun, and it was the perfect break from the hustle and bustle of Manhattan.

The atmosphere is unique – it almost feels European, with its narrow street, outdoor seating, and lively mix of locals, office workers, and tourists. Even at midday, the energy was great without being overwhelming. The service was friendly, the beers were cold, and it’s just a fantastic place to sit back, relax, and soak in the vibe of lower Manhattan.

Stone Street is especially worth visiting if you’re already in the area, for example exploring Battery Park, Wall Street, or the 9/11 Memorial. It’s not just about one bar – it’s the whole setting that makes it so special. Whether you’re looking for a casual drink, a hearty lunch, or just a place to people-watch, Stone Street delivers. A true New York experience that feels both historic and modern at the same time.

google_details.formatted_address: 83 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Mad Dog & Beans Mexican Cantina

reviews.text: Was in the area decided to treat myself for lunch w/ a margarita on a Rainy afternoon. The waitress was attentive & welcoming. The music was Mexican the food was Mexican on the menu but I could tell it was American Mexican I went with the steak medium rare Caesar w/ a side of refried beans.. the beans were good the salad was decent I like my lettuce to be more green but it was decent the steak was delicious. Now the margarita was the star of the show ! They have a full vast list of several tequilas on the menu .. I was relaxed & ready for the remainder of my day !

Been here twice now, once for drinks and once for actual food. I’m a fan of their frozen margaritas, seems like a solid price especially for the 33oz one. The al pastor tacos were also pretty tasty but I do wish they gave you more rice and beans with them, although can’t complain too much about them otherwise. Watched 1pm football here both times too and it hasn’t been loud at all either time, very quiet and easy to watch. Would recommend and will return for more margs!

Huge portions🌯 We sat on the patio along Stone St. - while the atmosphere was nice, the rest wasn’t.

For the price, the portions were worth it. As someone who loves Mexican food however, the food fell short. I’m not sure if it was the lack of flavour in the food… There was not event a hint of the limes or cilantro that usually have you nodding your head. Or it could be that the food didn’t taste fresh. The rice was bland, the pico unsavoury, and the refried beans tasted like they’d been pushed around all day.

Our server also wasn’t the best. I understand that we were seated on the patio and it takes a bit more patience to ask for water or napkins. But our server didn’t do so much as introduce himself. It almost felt like he was hiding from the patio section to avoid doing his job. He seemed annoyed when we asked for small things like hot sauce or an extra plate. We didn’t get our water topped off until we were finished and paying the bill.

I wouldn’t recommend this restaurant when visiting Stone St.. When I’m back in New York I’ll be sure to try the others in the area.

Had a great time here and absolutely demolished the chips and guacamole - maybe the best I’ve ever had. The enchiladas were also delicious. Service was quick and helpful. The inside of the restaurant was huge and very well decorated.

The food was just ok. The tacos come in 4 and you have to get all of them the same, kinda disappointing. Chips and queso and the queso seemed like the kind you buy in a grocery store. Chicken flautas had a very odd taste/texture it was like they ground up the chicken and mixed something in it. Chicken Caesar salad was probably the fav item at the table. Signature Margarita was perfect as well as our server.

google_details.formatted_address: 30 Water St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: The Dead Rabbit

reviews.text: A Christmas cracker! Even without the decorations we would be giving a 5 star review. Irish coffee is out of this world, it was the biggest recommendation from friends before going and it surpassed expectations. The shepherds pie was delicious and the beef sandwich too. What really made it different though was the service - really felt like the went the extra mile for their customers. Prepare for it to be busy though - we turned up at midday and it was full already!

What a wonderful Saturday brunch treat in this classic yet modern Irish pub. The decor is really cool along with signed dollar bills and photos around the walls and ceiling, Irish frozen coffee machine, etc.  Their cocktails are delicious and eye pleasing. Their authentic Irish fares and drinks were absolutely a delight. We had the Irish Breakfast, oysters, fried boneless chicken, ham and cheese toasties and chips, Irish frozen coffee and a perfectly pourer pint of Guinness.  We also went to Dead Rabbit at night before and they had really great Irish music as well 🥰

The Irish coffee is a must-try, you won’t be disappointed. The Old Fashioned was good, though a bit on the sweeter side for my taste. The fried chicken was just okay, nothing particularly special. The tiramisu was one of the best I’ve had. We sat in the Parlour, which had great vibes and good service, making for a pleasant overall experience. We did not have a reservation for parlor, but still managed to get a table on a Sunday.

Drank at the tap room.  I remember a time when the tap room only had beer. I am so pleased to know they have cocktails and food available now.

This place has always been impeccable.  I’ve been here a handful of times and never left disappointed.  I can’t remember the names of the drinks but they had a cocktail that had Guinness and it really was a standout, as well as the Irish coffee Alexander, and I don’t even like coffee 🤣.

Don’t sleep on the Scotch eggs either. Best ones I’ve had!  This one really upsets me that we don’t have something similar in Vegas.  I mean we have bars  but this one just has something about it 😁

My husband and I had an incredible time at The Dead Rabbit! It was a highlight of our bar crawl, and we were completely blown away by the unique experience. The place has a fantastic energy, and we loved that each floor offered a different vibe.
The cocktails were the star of the show. I had a special drink where the bartender spritzed a perfume on my hand to enhance the aroma as I sipped—a truly unforgettable sensory experience! The food was delicious, too; we especially enjoyed the deviled eggs. With amazing drinks, tasty bites, and a super cool atmosphere, The Dead Rabbit is a must-visit. We'll definitely be back!

google_details.formatted_address: 58 Stone St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Ulysses'

reviews.text: Had the best time here!! Great vibes for after work and for a regular night out. If you love an irish pub, you will love it here. Extra shout out to our bartender Simon. He made the night that much better. Was outgoing and conversational. The best of the best! He deserves a raise to say the least— he made the experience that much better.

It is located on Stone Street along with other bars and restaurants. Lots  of outdoor seating serving food and drinks it is perfect for summer afternoons or evenings. I was there on a Thursday evening listening to a good cover band. Most of the crowd was 25-30's, many coming from work. Fun place!

First time into this quaint but bustling Irish bar. I thought I might have a hard time getting decent service as there was plenty of seemingly regulars there. I’m happy to report the exact opposite. Our bartender Simon was incredibly attentive throughout the entire night. I truly felt like Simon took his time and gave me and my group an absolutely unique experience. 10/10 do recommend

Had such a good time at Ulysses, The bartender Simon was so much fun. If you ever want the best lamb stew, you’re in the right place. Can’t recommend this place enough! . Five stars because 10 is not an option!.

Nice place, nice beer, not cheap of course, look where you at haha, lot´s of space to sit, large bar, live music. Very cool.

And now I´ll gift you the best route to end up having a beer here after an awesome day of turism.

First Go to the highline, then stop at Chelsea Market, keep going down to Little Island, then cross to Whitney Museum, then take Hudson St and keep going down till you Grove St and see Friend´s Building, that´s a really cool route.

After, go to ghostbusters headquarters, which is very close by, then you do One World Trade Center and 9/11 Memorial.

Then do the Charging Bull and finish your route at Stone St. with a beautiful cold beer at Ulysees Bar.

Top notch route ;) You´re welcome.

google_details.formatted_address: 60 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Bombay's

reviews.text:
The place has aced the food for the soul. There is Americanized Indian food and then there is tonned down Indian food and this place has nailed the right flavor and texture that rekindles your memory when you sip the chai and take a bite of the samosa. Kudos to the team both at the the front end and in the backend. Would love to see this place prosper.

Ps- If you badly want a pallet refresh with the overdosed cheese and spices , this is your next stop.
Walk here right after the liberty visit

I was on New York trip with my husband  and we stayed in a hotel just 2 min. walk from Bombay's, Manhattan . We have been multiple times this place and tasted Lunch buffet and many other dishes. We liked all of the dishes. They don't disappoint  you. I tried chicken as well which was tasty. Only thing is they don't make Chai of my taste, but that's okay :)

Great Indian spot in Fidi! Nothing fancy just good, solid food and nice service. Their garlic naan was incredible and the chicken curry was juicy and flavorful. There was no wait when we arrived (albeit early dinner on a weekend) but the place definitely filled up as we got into the 6/6:30 range.

So freaking good and very accommodating to vegans :) They can make the dals, chana masala, jeera aloo, and more vegan. It’s always the food I crave most after coming home from a long trip. And we live nearby so the staff often deliver themselves — they are always so sweet. Truly a joy to have in the neighborhood!!

The food here tastes really good and authentic – definitely gives you that desi flavor you’re looking for in the city. The restaurant is cozy and nicely decorated, with a decent seating area. However, the prices are on the higher side, and they charged us an extra $15 as a “party fee” even though we were just 5 people, which felt a bit unreasonable. Worth trying for the food, but just be prepared for extra charges.

google_details.formatted_address: 100 Old Slip, New York, NY 10005, USA
google_details.name: Jiannetto's Pizza Truck

reviews.text: i got the penne vodka pasta with chicken and garlic knots. the pasta was great, creamy sauce, crispy chicken, though i wish they used grooved penne instead of a smooth one. it came out piping hot and with cheese on it. (he asked me first). the menu doesnt say the pasta comes with garlic knots but i asked to confirm. they gave me like 3, but honestly they were not good. they were like rock solid and not that flavorful, it was disappointing. but other than that, the service was prompt and helpful, i got everything i needed such as cutlery and a napkin. the price was pretty good for the portion. thank you!

This restaurant outperforms all similar places. The beverages showed creativity while the food showed skill. Good setting, excellent dishes, and helpful employees! I'll make Jiannetto's Pizza Truck a priority on my next visit. No other restaurant compares to this one.
Couldn't recommend more! The pizza van was the best addition to our Runna event, Jake was incredible and the pizza JUST WOW. Who knew grandma style could be so good!!!!
Beautiful and even better tasting pizza!

First time in Brooklyn visiting the brewery…and lucked out to have this wonderful pizza truck parked outside on a rainy Sunday evening.  Just giving it a shot was the best decision of the day because holiest of holy, this pizza is phenomenal.  It was the best pizza I had in 2022.  And 2021.  And 2020.

Huge shoutout to the owner for hooking us up on our second round for our trip back to Manhattan.

google_details.formatted_address: 79 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Route 66 Smokehouse

reviews.text:
$25 p/p bottomless brunch drinks for 90 minutes!!! Best part is that the drinks are actually good.

Came across this place on a Sunday walking by stone street, it was a nice day out and their outdoor sitting is really comfortable.

The service was great and really fast too.

The ribs were mostly on the dry tough side but they were still tasty.

The start of this meal was the garlic shrimp, I’m planning on going back just for that dish. The sauce was so delicious I almost wanted to just order another one and forget about the ribs.

Overall great experience.

Great spot to hang out with friends over a few beers! 🍺 The atmosphere is lively but still comfortable, and they have a wide selection of craft beers on tap. The staff was friendly and helpful when recommending drinks. The food portions were generous and paired well with the beer, especially the fried chicken.

One small downside was that it got a bit noisy during peak hours, so it might not be the best place if you’re looking for a quiet conversation. Overall, a fun and enjoyable place for casual drinks and snacks. I’ll definitely come back!

The server was super sweet and the food came out fast but the ribs were dry and not too tender. It's barely a smokehouse, not many smoked options. My boyfriend enjoyed his steak sandwich, he said it was juicy. The chicken wings were really good though, tasty and juicy. Also, the high top bench leather seating was impossible to sit on, I kept slipping off. The gaming aspect is advertised but a lot of machines were down and the darts were by people eating so I couldn't do that. Super disappointing.

I recently had a delightful experience at Route 66 Smokehouse. Opting for the New York Strip Steak, I was thoroughly impressed with the quality and flavor. It was cooked perfectly to my liking and had a rich, savory taste that really hit the spot.

My wife chose the Smoked Baby-Back Ribs, and she couldn't stop raving about how tender and flavorful they were. The smokiness was just right, and the meat practically fell off the bone.

The atmosphere was welcoming and the service attentive without being overbearing. Overall, we had a very satisfying meal and would definitely recommend Route 66 Smokehouse for anyone looking for well-prepared American cuisine.

Showed up with 6 adults and 4 small children, in NYC that's insane to try and eat somewhere but this place accommodated us.  Everyone who waited on us was super nice, understanding with the kids, very professional.  I absolutely would recommend this place and the top floor is a nice spot when it fully opens for adults.  Food was good!  Got the breakfast burger and it was tasty.

google_details.formatted_address: 25 Bridge St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: White Horse Tavern - Financial District

reviews.text: Very busy pub with great atmosphere and good pub fare.  The staff is friendly and the vibe is half locals half tourists.

The White Horse Tavern in FiDi is our favorite pub. The atmosphere is excellent. Sam and Tomas are such kind, accommodating, and generous people that it's worth coming here just for them. The drinks are also excellent and reasonably priced. The food is always delicious and fresh. You always feel very comfortable and well looked after. The whole staff is attentive and helpful without ever feeling pressing or overbearing. Will definitely be back! Highly recommended.
Very nice and comfortable place to have some food!and expecially not expensive!spent 52$ for what you can see in my pictures!
Fantastic mood and atmosphere, big dose of food, foods are tasty, polite service, good beers.... Recommended tavern!

Fantastic, small, local owned tavern with great food and drinks. The burgers were great! The pita and hummus was good too!  Very small but really delicious food and great service. They sat my family of 5 quickly and food came out quick!

google_details.formatted_address: 26 S William St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Luke's Lobster FiDi

reviews.text: Stopped in for a quick dinner, there was no wait to order and the food came out quickly. I ordered a cup of clam chowder and the flight trio which is 1 roll of lobster, crab and shrimp. They are about half the size of a full roll. You get a bag of chips and a drink included when you order rolls. If you eat there they bring your food out to you. The seafood was super fresh and delicious.

We stopped by Luke’s Lobster in New York right after our Ellis Island and Statue of Liberty tour and it did not disappoint! We tried the lobster, crab, and shrimp rolls, and each one was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly portioned. The toasted buns were buttery and crisp—absolutely delicious. We also had the clam chowder, which might just be the best we’ve ever had—rich, creamy, and full of flavor. A must-visit spot for seafood lovers in the city!

Luke’s Lobster was… fine. The lobster roll was small but decent, nothing special. The $35 price tag felt steep, especially since it only came with a bag of chips and a store-bought drink. For the same price, you could easily get several better meals nearby. Not terrible, just not worth the cost.
Pure decadence, but so much sweet buttery lobster in one roll is something I'll never forget. Definitely worth it to try once. Staff was lovely.
Me and my friend order seafood roll fight!$31! Those seafood are fresh and delicious 🤤 my favorite is lobster 🦞

google_details.formatted_address: 90 Broad St G103, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Potbelly

reviews.text: I’m happy to say I’d be doing business with Potbelly for every office event that requires catering! My team and I hosted an event today and I had the pleasure of organizing the perfect Luncheon thanks to Shanta and Claire! This Location has a great team. They packaged our Large bundle for 10 perfectly with ample condiments, and a great assortment of chips and cookies. A 10/10 experience, I highly recommend for your next office gathering, charitable event or conference meeting!

They’re located at 90 Broad Street G103, Financial District in Lower Manhattan, New York, NY. I placed an online order and delivery was quick since they’re right downstairs from us. They have always given great service and delicious subs, not to mention their milkshakes and cookies.

I visit this location every single day and i must say reading these reviews i really think people just don’t appreciate this staff here, the management team itself amazing you can tell that Shanta makes sure she sets her team up for the day. I speak to her everyday when I’m there and she’s always smiling and putting her team on point, her managers under her you can tell they have the up most respect for her because when she’s out it still feel like she’s there. Everyone at this location deserves a raise and some more. Day after day they manage to handle huge amount of catering, i one time spoke to Shanta and she stated she was in at 3-4 am and her shift didn’t start until 8am and was not off work til 7pm i don’t know about anyone else but that’s dedication. Please get to know the staff before you judge them you don’t know the day they may have had. People should understand “CUSTOMERS ARE NOT ALWAYS RIGHT” the food is always fresh, hot and tasty. Don’t listen and read the reviews people lie when they don’t get their way. I’ve witnessed customers get nasty and rude for no reason and i bet it’s because people read these reviews. The shop is always clean from the walls to the floors and restroom by far the cleanest location out of all of the I’ve visited. All i can say is keep up the good work. If i was to ever walk in and hear  Shanta or the others no longer work there ill stop eating at potbelly for good.

An amazing sandwich shop with great choices of pre selected sandwiches and the option of building your own from scratch. You can choose 3 different sizes. The staff prepare your sandwich in front of you and are very generous with the fillings. There's plenty of indoor seating too.

Food is good but the workers are rude and have an attitude. I get its high volume, but they could be a little bit nicer. I got attitude three times in a row for no reason… I will not be returning because of it….

google_details.formatted_address: 1 New York Plz, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Chipotle Mexican Grill

reviews.text: I had the carne asada burrito and it was very good. Customer service was fast.

Had to try Chipotle after the many recommendations. Had the Burrito Bowl with everything. Not the biggest fan of their hot salsa. The portion was huge and the food was okay. Lots of options for vegetarians at budget friendly prices and decent flavors. Also lots of branches of Chipotle across NYC. Definitely makes for a great meal option, while on the go!

Really? After seeing the news recently that all the employees are trained to give proper portion of protein, I went back to actually see, but I am super disappointed that I am given half scoop of chicken in my $14 bowl. Absolutely nasty attitudes of all employees. Compared to ADOBO, adobo is 1000 X times better, but they don't have a location in Manhattan.

I go to chipotle at least once a week and always had a decent experience until now. 9/2/25 around 12:50-1pm
Ordered a carnitas bowl which I was given small scraps of meet from the bottom of a clearly already empty tin. I told him that I wanted a normal portion for my meat which the guy serving me acted frustrated, proceeds to continue to pick up an extra strand from an already empty tin and rushed me to the next station. I would have stepped back and waited for the refill if necessary. Unacceptable

Food was okay, vegetarian options available, the place was clean and quite empty so there was no problem with seats - we came here on a weekday, an October afternoon. Service was super nice - we remember very fondly the guy who was selling us our meals as we had really nice talk with him!

google_details.formatted_address: 28 Water St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Subway

reviews.text: I must say, this subway location is one of a kind. There is one single man running the show and he prepared my sandwich with the utmost care, even with a line of people behind of me. He then took care of other patrons with the same care and effort.

When arriving to pick up my online order they informed me that the drink machine was down so they would have to refund my drink, which was understandable but after getting home my entire order was made incorrectly. No lettuce in my salad, no black olives, the tomatoes were soggy and obviously past their line time. With it being a such a slow business day, you would expect the workers to be able to read a receipt and make an order correct.

The store itself is fine enough, the staff is the real problem. While they were decent with me they burnt the churro, the sandwich had a pitiful amount of meat, the sandwich price was ridiculous (tho I know that can be a Subway in general issue) and they were yelling at an employee for making the most minor of mistakes. It's an almost passable place to get a sandwich but with so many better options in the neighborhood (there's like 3 other better Subways in spitting distance) it's so much worse in comparison.

amazing staff, super down to earth and felt welcomed, made an amazing quality sandwich with turkey breast for me. I needed directions around NYC and the employee really helped me with that and took his time with it too. overall a superb experience
Fast and nice serving . Delicious

With it being a such a slow business day, you would expect the workers to be able to read a receipt and make an order correct.

google_details.formatted_address: 60 Pearl St, New York, NY 10004, USA
google_details.name: Aahar

reviews.text:
They are renamed to Bombay  Indian Restaurant.
Food quality is best in NYC and their taste cant be beat in the city.. wonderful dinning experience..

This was one of the best meals we had during our stay in NYC. We had the chicken tandoori which was so tender and well spiced, the mattar paneer also very well done, garlic naan and mango lassi. We were so happy w our meal and pleasantly surprised by the great prices and generous portions.

We enjoyed a delicious dinner here. The food was incredible - fresh and filled with flavor.
We ordered vegetable samosas, chicken kebobs, Aloo Gobi, Bhel Puri,  and Lamb Vindaloo. All were fantastic. The staff was so friendly, and the gentleman that came around to ensure we were enjoying our experience was the sweetest. Although I didn’t necessarily find it to be the nicest dining experience from an aesthetic point of view, (the decor could be nicer) the food was phenomenal. We will definitely be back!!

This is the second time I've been here because the amount of food you get for the price makes it worth it. It's decent food at an affordable price that will leave you feeling satisfied. The staff have been super friendly and attentive to my needs both times I have visited

I went for the pre-fix lunch special and got an order of Butter Chicken with aromatic basmati rice, Cocktail samosa (veggie), Garlic naan, and Mango Lassi for under $25.

Food:
Butter Chicken: I told the waiter to make it on the spicier side when I placed my order but I received the opposite. It was on the sweeter side which was okay but just a little disappointing. The butter chicken was flavorful and had a nice creamy mouth feel. I did find it to be more on the tomato-y side which overpowered the rest of the spices. The chicken wasn't dry and had great flavors. It was pretty decent but again I wish it had a little more heat to it. The basmati rice - although fragrant and tasty it could have been a bit softer with a little more moisture. 4.5/5

Garlic Naan: By far one of the best garlic naan I've tasted in a very long time. The dough was fully cooked through, super soft with crispy pockets, and the garlic was not too overpowering.  5/5

Cocktail Samosa: The exterior was light, crispy, and golden to perfection. The insides were stuffed with lightly seasoned potatoes and peas. The stuffing could have used more seasoning, although I did taste the cardamon a lot of the other spices didn't come through. What really saved this appetizer for me was the mint sauce (hari sauce), it was bright, fresh, and brought it all together. The tamarind sauce was too sweet for me and just sort of sat there. 4/5

Mango Lassi: This drink was sweet, sour, and extra thiccc. It was creamy and delicious, but it did take me at least 10 minutes to get it all down. Luckily I had a glass of water near by to help it go down with ease. 4/5

Service:
Very friendly staff that will check in on you once in a little while to make sure everything is to your liking.

Decor:
Dimly lit restaurant with a clean and cozy atmosphere. Plenty of space for large and small groups. The restrooms were cleaned and well maintained.

Overall I did enjoy my food, I left with a full belly and satisfied with my order. The prices are affordable for the area and the food is decent.

I'll def visit Aahar again!
Very tasty. The place had lots of room for a late dinner. The food was great and the waiter pointed out some fun dishes to try to expand the usual set of options. They were all delicious.
